title: Spark Change-Makers Project: Comprehensive Guide to the Youth-Led & Youth-Focused CSO/Social Enterprise Application (Cohort 1)
summary: The Spark Change-Makers Project, part of the EU–Ghana Youth Empowerment Programme, is a major initiative designed to strengthen youth-led and youth-focused Civil Society Organisations (CSOs) and Social Enterprises across Ghana. Implemented by SOS Children’s Villages Ghana in partnership with Songtaba and Social Innovation Africa, this programme equips young leaders with the skills, knowledge, and resources needed to improve livelihoods and drive sustainable development. This article provides a detailed breakdown of the application form, eligibility requirements, thematic focus areas, and expectations for organisations seeking to join Cohort 1 of the capacity-strengthening phase. Overview of the Spark Change-Makers Project According to the document: “The Spark Change-makers Project is a European Union co-funded initiative designed to empower young people (18-35) by equipping them with skills, knowledge, and resources to improve their livelihoods and strengthen their participation in sustainable development.” The project operates across six regions: Greater Accra Ashanti Volta Northern Western Upper East Eligible organisations may progress to a grant-support phase after completing the capacity-strengthening stage. Eligibility Summary To qualify, organisations must meet the following criteria: Must be youth-led, youth-focused, or a social enterprise. Must operate in at least one of the six implementing regions. Must demonstrate strong community engagement. Must show commitment to inclusion, sustainability, and environmentally friendly solutions. Section-by-Section Breakdown of the Application Form SECTION A: Introduction Consent Applicants provide basic organisational details including: Name of organisation Region of operation Community name Organisation email Primary contact person Phone number Type of organisation (CSO or Social Enterprise) Youth leadership/focus category SECTION B: Organisational Profile Legal Status Options Registered Not registered Informally organised and operating for at least 12 months Mission/Goal of the Organisation Applicants must clearly state their mission and long-term goals. Organisational Documents Systems Applicants indicate whether they possess: Strategic Plan Constitution or By-laws Financial Policy Prior donor funding Monitoring Evaluation system Thematic Areas (Must Select at Least One) Organisations must directly address at least one of the following themes: Digital Technology Gender Youth Empowerment Culture Creative Industries Agriculture Environmental Protection Peace Security Applicants must provide one concrete example of work done in the selected theme(s). Youth Leadership Requirements The document states: “The CEO / Executive Director must be aged 18-35, OR the majority of the Management must be aged 18-35, OR the organisation s primary mission and at least 80% of its activities must directly benefit young people aged 18-35.” Applicants must also provide statistics on: Board members Staff Youth staff Youth in leadership SECTION C: Experience Impact Applicants must describe: Key activities completed in the last year Number of beneficiaries served Evidence of impact (reports, documentation, digital content) Social media handles SECTION D: Thematic Focus Community Impact Applicants must explain: The specific social problem addressed Primary beneficiaries and their community Significant activities completed in the last 12 months Why the community needs the organisation’s work Sustainability measures SECTION G: Capacity Needs Applicants must identify: Three major capacity gaps How the Spark Change-Makers Project will address these gaps Sustainability plans after the project ends SECTION H: Inclusion Equity Applicants indicate whether they serve: Young women Young people with disabilities Urban or rural communities Environmental sustainability Safeguarding policies They must also describe how their work promotes gender equality and addresses discrimination. SECTION J: Regional Validation Applicants confirm: Primary region of operation Whether they operate in multiple regions SECTION K: Declaration Applicants must confirm the accuracy of their information and agree to project terms. The document states: “Submitting this application does not guarantee selection or grant funding.” They must also confirm compliance with EU grant regulations and SOS CV Ghana’s data protection policy. Deadline Application Deadline: 31st July 2026 For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more training opportunities here The post Spark Change-Makers Project: Comprehensive Guide to the Youth-Led Youth-Focused CSO/Social Enterprise Application (Cohort 1) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNICEF StartUp Lab Reducing Single-Use Plastics through WASH & Social and Behaviour Change Innovation Challenge 2026: Win $5,000, Pilot Your Innovation in Ghana, and Help Protect Children’s Health
summary: Applications are now open for the UNICEF StartUp Lab Reducing Single-Use Plastics through Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) and Social Behaviour Change (SBC) Innovation Challenge 2026 . This innovation challenge is inviting startups, innovators, entrepreneurs, social enterprises, and changemakers to develop practical, scalable, and sustainable solutions that reduce the use of single-use plastics within Ghana s WASH sector while promoting healthier communities and protecting children from the harmful effects of plastic pollution. Selected innovators will receive funding, mentorship, technical support, and the opportunity to pilot their solutions within UNICEF WASH and community programmes. Application Deadline: July 23, 2026 About the UNICEF StartUp Lab WASH Innovation Challenge Plastic pollution has become one of the biggest environmental and public health challenges affecting Ghana today. Single-use plastics are widely used across the country, particularly within water, sanitation, and hygiene services. Products such as: Plastic water sachets Plastic packaging Disposable hygiene products Other single-use plastic materials continue to accumulate in communities due to inadequate collection and disposal systems. Improper disposal of these plastics blocks drainage systems, pollutes rivers and streams, contaminates soil, and contributes significantly to greenhouse gas emissions. The challenge goes beyond environmental concerns. Poor waste management creates stagnant water that becomes breeding grounds for mosquitoes, increasing the spread of malaria and other water-borne diseases. Burning plastic waste also contributes to poor air quality, increasing respiratory illnesses among vulnerable populations, particularly children. As plastics gradually degrade into microplastics, they contaminate water sources and food systems, posing long-term risks to public health and food security. Recognizing these growing challenges, UNICEF StartUp Lab is seeking innovative solutions that promote a circular economy while transforming how communities use, collect, reuse, replace, reduce, and recycle plastics. Why This Challenge Matters Current plastic waste management systems are not sufficient to address the growing environmental crisis. The UNICEF StartUp Lab seeks to encourage innovative approaches that move communities away from the traditional use-and-dispose model toward sustainable circular systems. The challenge combines innovation with Social and Behaviour Change (SBC) approaches to encourage lasting behavioural shifts that reduce dependence on disposable plastics. Ultimately, the initiative aims to improve sanitation systems, protect the environment, strengthen climate resilience, and improve health outcomes for children. Objectives of the Challenge The challenge seeks innovative solutions that can: Reduce dependence on single-use plastics. Promote responsible plastic collection and disposal. Encourage reuse, reduction, replacement, and recycling using the 3Rs. Improve sanitation and hygiene services. Strengthen community participation in sustainable waste management. Protect children from diseases associated with plastic pollution. Support climate-resilient WASH systems. Develop scalable and affordable innovations that can be implemented across Ghana. Challenge Themes Applicants may address one or more of the following innovation areas. 1. Alternative Solutions Develop affordable, accessible, and scalable alternatives to single-use plastics used within water, sanitation, and hygiene services. Solutions should promote: Reduction Replacement Reuse Recycling while remaining practical for local communities. 2. Behavioural Integration Design Social and Behaviour Change (SBC) strategies that encourage households, institutions, and communities to reduce their reliance on disposable plastics. Innovations may include: Behaviour change campaigns Digital engagement platforms Community education programmes Incentive systems School-based interventions 3. Circular Systems Develop closed-loop systems that improve the collection, reuse, reduction, replacement, recycling, or responsible disposal of plastics throughout the WASH value chain. Potential innovations may include: Plastic collection systems Community recycling programmes Deposit-return schemes Incentive-based recycling models Waste recovery technologies Circular business models 4. Climate Resilience Create innovative WASH infrastructure and services capable of reducing the environmental impacts of plastic pollution while protecting communities from climate-related risks. Solutions should improve both environmental sustainability and child health. What UNICEF Is Looking For UNICEF is seeking practical innovations capable of significantly reducing or eliminating single-use plastics from household and community environments. Successful applicants should demonstrate their ability to: Develop Sustainable Alternatives Innovations should introduce practical alternatives to disposable plastics that are affordable, locally available, and environmentally sustainable. Drive Behavioural Change Applicants should demonstrate how Social and Behaviour Change methodologies will encourage communities and service providers to adopt sustainable practices. Improve Community Health Solutions should reduce children s exposure to pollution caused by plastic waste while supporting healthier sanitation systems. Deliver Scalable Models Innovations should be: Affordable Scalable Adaptable Easy to integrate into existing government or community WASH programmes Benefits for Selected Innovators Successful applicants will receive: US$5,000 cash prize Opportunity to pilot their innovation within UNICEF WASH and community programmes Mentorship from experts Data support to strengthen and validate their innovation Opportunity to build scalable and cost-effective solutions Alignment with Ghana s National Plastic Management Policy Access to public-sector WASH implementation opportunities Strategic Importance This challenge supports several national and global development priorities, including: Ghana s National Plastic Management Policy Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 6 – Clean Water and Sanitation Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 13 – Climate Action The programme also strengthens collaboration between startups, innovators, government institutions, and development partners to create sustainable environmental solutions that directly improve children s wellbeing. Expected Outcomes Selected innovations will be piloted within existing UNICEF WASH programmes and community initiatives. The pilot projects will generate evidence demonstrating how innovative approaches can: Reduce plastic waste. Improve waste collection systems. Encourage behavioural change. Strengthen sanitation infrastructure. Improve environmental sustainability. Protect children s health. Create scalable models for wider implementation across Ghana. The long-term vision is to build a portfolio of proven innovations capable of transforming plastic waste management while creating healthier communities and more resilient WASH systems. Why You Should Apply If your startup or innovation focuses on sustainability, waste management, climate action, circular economy, sanitation, recycling, behaviour change, environmental technology, or WASH, this challenge offers an excellent opportunity to test your innovation in a real-world environment. Beyond the financial award, successful innovators will gain valuable pilot experience, expert mentorship, technical support, and the opportunity to contribute to national environmental priorities while improving the lives of children across Ghana. Application Deadline July 23, 2026 How to Apply Interested applicants can apply for the UNICEF StartUp Lab HERE . Click HERE for more information on the UNICEF StartUp Lab. Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here . The post UNICEF StartUp Lab Reducing Single-Use Plastics through WASH Social and Behaviour Change Innovation Challenge 2026: Win $5,000, Pilot Your Innovation in Ghana, and Help Protect Children s Health appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully‑Funded PhD Program in Biomedical Sciences at the Max Delbrück Center (MDC), Berlin
summary: The Max Delbrück Center (MDC) in Berlin stands as one of Europe’s leading research institutions in biomedical sciences, offering an exceptional, fully‑funded PhD program designed for ambitious young scientists who want to explore the foundations of human health and disease. With cutting‑edge facilities, world‑class mentors, and a deeply collaborative research culture, the MDC PhD Program provides an outstanding environment for scientific growth and career development. Program Overview The MDC PhD Program is a structured, research‑intensive doctoral training pathway that supports students in conducting innovative biomedical research across multiple disciplines. The program focuses on three major scientific themes: Genes, Cells and Cell‑based Medicine Molecular Processes and Therapies Integrative Biomedicine These themes reflect MDC’s mission to advance tomorrow’s medicine through discovery, translational science, and interdisciplinary collaboration. What the Program Offers Doctoral researchers at MDC benefit from a comprehensive training ecosystem designed to support both scientific excellence and personal development. Key features include: International Research Environment — Work alongside scientists from more than 70 countries. Individualized Supervision Mentoring — Each student receives guidance from a dedicated thesis committee. Advanced Scientific Training — Access to workshops, lectures, journal clubs, and specialized courses. State‑of‑the‑Art Infrastructure — Cutting‑edge laboratories, core facilities, and technology platforms. Participation in Specialized Research Programs — Including MDC‑BIMSB, ECRC, and HI‑TAC. Career Development Support — Structured programs for leadership, communication, and scientific growth. Travel Grants — Funding for external conferences, workshops, and courses. Active Alumni Network — Long‑term professional connections worldwide. Optional Enrollment in HEIBRiDS Data Science School — For students interested in computational biology and AI. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must hold (or expect to obtain within four months after the interview week) a Master’s degree or equivalent in one of the following fields: Biology Biotechnology Medicine Molecular Medicine Chemistry Computer Science Data Science Engineering Mathematics Physics Or a closely related discipline Applicants should demonstrate strong motivation for biomedical research and readiness to engage in interdisciplinary scientific work. Application Process The MDC conducts central recruitment once every Fall , and applications are accepted exclusively through the online portal. Application Steps Register on the Application Portal Submit required documents (CV, transcripts, motivation letter, references). Select preferred research topics and group leaders. Participate in online interviews (October). Successful candidates begin their PhD in January , with flexibility up to four months after interviews . Deadline: The next call opens June 29, 2026 , and remains open through August 2026. Application deadline: August 24, 2026 Funding Employment Doctoral researchers receive: A fully‑funded 3‑year employment contract (often extended to 4 years). Salary according to 65% TVöD 13 , approximately 1800 EUR net per month . Full health and social insurance coverage. Access to university enrollment with semester fees of 300–320 EUR . Available Projects A complete list of participating group leaders and project descriptions becomes accessible upon registration in the application portal. Projects span molecular biology, genomics, computational biology, cardiovascular research, regenerative medicine, and more. Timeline (Approximate) Call Open: June – August Online Interviews: October Start Date: January (or up to 4 months after interviews) Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post Fully‑Funded PhD Program in Biomedical Sciences at the Max Delbrück Center (MDC), Berlin appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AWLN 8th Intergenerational Retreat 2026 in Gabon: Fully Sponsored Leadership Opportunity for Young African Women Leaders and Diaspora Changemakers
summary: The African Women Leaders Network (AWLN), jointly supported by the African Union (AU) and the United Nations (UN), has officially opened applications for its prestigious 8th Intergenerational Retreat (IGR 8) . This flagship leadership programme will bring together 40 outstanding young African women leaders from across the African continent and the Diaspora for a fully sponsored retreat in Libreville, Gabon , in December 2026. Designed as a platform for mentorship, leadership development, networking, and collaboration, the AWLN Intergenerational Retreat connects emerging women leaders with influential policymakers, Heads of State, business executives, and experienced leaders who are shaping Africa s future. The retreat aligns with the African Union s Agenda 2063 and the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), strengthening women s leadership and accelerating gender equality, peace, governance, and sustainable development across Africa. Application Deadline: 03 August 2026 About the African Women Leaders Network (AWLN) The African Women Leaders Network (AWLN) is a joint initiative of the African Union (AU) and the United Nations (UN) that seeks to increase women s leadership and participation in peacebuilding, governance, economic development, and sustainable development across Africa. Since its establishment, AWLN has built a powerful network of women leaders spanning government, diplomacy, civil society, academia, entrepreneurship, youth movements, and international organizations. The network promotes intergenerational dialogue, women s empowerment, mentorship, and collaborative leadership to address Africa s most pressing social, economic, and political challenges. About the AWLN 8th Intergenerational Retreat (IGR 8) The Intergenerational Retreat is AWLN s signature leadership programme that brings together established and emerging women leaders to exchange ideas, strengthen leadership skills, and build meaningful partnerships. Rather than functioning as a traditional conference, the retreat serves as a launchpad for long-term collaboration, mentorship, and action-oriented leadership. The 2026 edition will take place in Libreville, Gabon , bringing together 40 selected young women leaders from across Africa and the African Diaspora. Participants will engage in interactive sessions, mentoring activities, leadership dialogues, collaborative workshops, and networking opportunities aimed at strengthening women s leadership across generations. Why the AWLN Intergenerational Retreat Matters Women continue to play a transformative role in advancing peace, governance, economic growth, climate action, entrepreneurship, and community development across Africa. However, many emerging women leaders face challenges such as limited access to mentorship, leadership opportunities, decision-making spaces, and regional networks. The AWLN Intergenerational Retreat addresses these challenges by creating a platform where experienced African women leaders can mentor and support the next generation of changemakers. The programme contributes directly to: Advancing women s leadership across Africa. Promoting gender equality and inclusion. Strengthening regional collaboration. Supporting young women in influencing policy and governance. Building sustainable leadership pipelines. Encouraging innovation and community impact. Objectives of the Retreat The AWLN 8th Intergenerational Retreat aims to: Strengthen women s leadership across Africa. Build the leadership capacity of emerging women leaders. Promote mentorship between senior and young women leaders. Foster intergenerational dialogue. Encourage collaboration across sectors and countries. Support implementation of Agenda 2063 and the Sustainable Development Goals. Create lasting partnerships that contribute to Africa s transformation. What Participants Will Gain Successful applicants will receive a fully sponsored opportunity to participate in one of Africa s leading women leadership programmes. Benefits include: 1. Fully Sponsored Participation Selected participants will receive: Full registration International or regional travel support Accommodation throughout the retreat This ensures participants can fully engage in the programme without financial barriers. 2. Mentorship from Influential Women Leaders Participants will interact directly with high-profile women leaders, including: Heads of State Vice Presidents Ministers CEOs Senior policymakers Development leaders Influential African women leaders These mentoring opportunities provide practical guidance, leadership insights, and career inspiration. 3. Leadership Development Participants will strengthen skills in areas such as: Strategic leadership Public speaking Policy engagement Advocacy Decision-making Collaboration Negotiation Community leadership 4. Pan-African Networking The retreat provides access to an extensive network of women leaders from: Governments Civil society International organizations Academia Private sector Youth movements African Diaspora communities Participants will establish professional relationships that extend well beyond the retreat. 5. AWLN Leadership Award Selected participants will also have the opportunity to receive the prestigious AWLN Leadership Award, recognizing outstanding commitment to leadership and community impact. 6. Continued Support Participants become part of AWLN s growing community and benefit from ongoing engagement through: Networking opportunities Leadership initiatives Knowledge sharing Mentorship Future regional collaborations Retreat Themes The programme focuses on strengthening leadership across AWLN s six strategic pillars: 1. Peace and Security Leadership initiatives that contribute to conflict prevention, peacebuilding, and security. 2. Governance and Political Participation Promoting women s participation in governance, leadership, policymaking, and democratic institutions. 3. Financial Inclusion, Economic Development, Natural Resources and Mining Supporting women s economic empowerment, entrepreneurship, financial inclusion, and sustainable resource management. 4. Agriculture, Rural Economy and Land Strengthening women s leadership in agriculture, food systems, rural development, and land governance. 5. Social Mobilisation Encouraging community engagement, advocacy, civic participation, and social transformation. 6. Youth and Emerging Leaders Empowering young women to become influential leaders across Africa. Who Should Apply? The programme seeks dynamic young African women leaders who have already demonstrated positive impact within their communities. Applicants should be passionate about creating lasting change and advancing women s leadership. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be aged between 18 and 45 years . Be an African woman residing on the continent or in the African Diaspora. Demonstrate measurable or recognized impact in their community. Be actively involved in one or more of AWLN s six thematic pillars. Show commitment to leadership, collaboration, and community development. What AWLN Expects from Participants The retreat is designed to create long-term impact rather than simply host an event. Selected participants are expected to: Participate actively in all sessions. Contribute ideas and experiences. Support intergenerational dialogue. Build meaningful collaborations. Promote AWLN s mission within their communities. Apply lessons learned after the retreat. Continue contributing to the AWLN network beyond the programme. AWLN emphasizes that participants are selected not only because of their achievements, but also because of their willingness to inspire others and contribute to Africa s collective development. Why You Should Apply The AWLN Intergenerational Retreat is one of the continent s premier leadership programmes for emerging African women leaders. Successful participants will have the opportunity to: Learn directly from influential African women leaders. Expand professional and regional networks. Strengthen leadership capacity. Gain international visibility. Collaborate with women leaders from across Africa and the Diaspora. Influence conversations on Africa s future. Contribute to advancing gender equality and sustainable development. For young women committed to creating meaningful change, the retreat provides a powerful platform to accelerate both personal and professional growth. Application Process Interested applicants should submit their applications online through the Official AWLN Application Platform . Applicants are encouraged to ensure that: All required information is completed accurately. Applications are submitted before the deadline. Supporting information clearly demonstrates leadership achievements and community impact. Only complete applications will be reviewed. Application Deadline Application Deadline: 03 August 2026 Late or incomplete applications may not be considered. How to Apply Applicants should: Visit the Official AWLN application Portal . Complete the online application form. Submit all required information before 03 August 2026 . Await communication from the AWLN Secretariat regarding the selection process. For additional information or inquiries, applicants may visit the AWLN official website HERE . Conclusion The AWLN 8th Intergenerational Retreat 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for young African women leaders to strengthen their leadership abilities, expand their regional and international networks, and collaborate with some of Africa s most influential women leaders. By bringing together emerging changemakers and experienced decision-makers, the retreat creates a powerful environment for mentorship, innovation, and collective action that supports Africa s long-term development goals. For women who are passionate about governance, peacebuilding, entrepreneurship, agriculture, social mobilisation, community development, or youth leadership, this fully sponsored programme provides an invaluable platform to grow, inspire others, and contribute meaningfully to the continent s future. Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications early and submit them before the 03 August 2026 deadline. Discover more global African , Fellowship , Women , and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY. The post AWLN 8th Intergenerational Retreat 2026 in Gabon: Fully Sponsored Leadership Opportunity for Young African Women Leaders and Diaspora Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: University of Messina PhD Scholarships 2026–2027 in Italy | Fully Funded & Funded Doctoral Programs for International Students Across 15 Research Fields
summary: The University of Messina has officially opened applications for its 42nd Cycle PhD Programs (Academic Year 2026/2027) , offering an exceptional opportunity for graduates from around the world to pursue doctoral studies in one of Italy s historic public universities. The competition invites applications for 15 doctoral (PhD) programmes covering engineering, medicine, economics, psychology, political science, sustainability, humanities, chemistry, veterinary sciences, cognitive sciences, law, public administration, physics, biology, and many other disciplines. Numerous fully funded PhD scholarships , executive doctoral positions, industry-funded research scholarships, and non-scholarship places are available. Successful candidates will undertake a three-year doctoral programme beginning on 1 November 2026 , conducting advanced research under internationally recognized academic supervisors while contributing to innovative scientific and societal solutions. The University of Messina welcomes applications from Italian and international candidates and offers several funding opportunities supported by the University, the Italian Ministry of University and Research (MUR), private companies, national agencies, and regional funding programmes. About the University of Messina PhD Programs The University of Messina is one of Italy s leading public universities with a strong international reputation in research, innovation, medicine, engineering, humanities, social sciences, and environmental studies. For the 2026/2027 academic year , the university is opening its 42nd Cycle of PhD programmes , providing candidates with opportunities to: Conduct cutting-edge scientific research Work alongside internationally recognized professors Collaborate with research institutes and private industries Participate in international mobility programmes Receive generous doctoral scholarships Build careers in academia, research, government, NGOs, healthcare, and industry The programmes officially commence on 1 November 2026 and run for three academic years . Application Deadline Application Deadline: 24 July 2026 (11:59 PM Italy Time) Applicants must complete and submit their online application before the deadline through the University s Esse3 platform. Late applications will not be accepted. Duration The PhD programmes last: Three years Starting on 1 November 2026 Available PhD Programmes Applications are open for the following doctoral programmes. 1. Bioengineering Applied to Medical Sciences Research areas include: Biomedical engineering Medical technologies Medical imaging Artificial intelligence in healthcare Dentistry Biotechnology Biomedical innovation Funding includes: 4 University/MUR scholarships 1 position without scholarship 2. Applied Biology and Experimental Medicine Curricula include: Experimental Medicine Biological and Environmental Sciences Pharmaceutical Sciences Funding: 4 scholarships 1 non-funded position 3. Economics, Management and Statistics Curricula include: Economics Management Statistics Special opportunities include: Executive PhD positions AI for biomedical management Sustainable innovation Circular economy research Corporate sustainability Industry partners include: Biofuture Medicine DPT Consulting Funding includes: 4 scholarships Executive industry PhD opportunities 2 non-funded positions 4. Physics Research areas include: Quantum physics Materials Astrophysics Experimental physics Theoretical physics Funding: 4 scholarships 1 non-funded position 5. Engineering One of the largest doctoral programmes available. Research areas include: Mechanical Engineering Computer Engineering Civil Engineering Electrical Engineering Cybersecurity Artificial Intelligence Naval Engineering Renewable Energy Hydrogen Technology Materials Engineering Telecommunications Aerospace Engineering Environmental Engineering Industry-funded scholarships are available through: National Cybersecurity Agency Novamarine Vishay Semiconductor Zappettificio Muzzi HYDRAE Research Project Several scholarships include: International mobility Industry placements Company-based research Overseas research periods 6. Psychology Research focuses include: Clinical Psychology Educational Psychology Cognitive Psychology Health Sciences Rehabilitation Funding: University scholarships University of Catanzaro scholarships 7. Chemical Sciences Research areas include: Pharmaceutical Chemistry Food Chemistry Molecular Systems Functional Materials Nutraceuticals Funding: 4 scholarships 1 non-funded position Additional PhD Programmes The call also includes doctoral opportunities in: Cognitive Sciences Public Administration Sciences Legal Sciences Political Science Humanities Veterinary, Biotechnological and Agricultural Sciences Sustainability Sciences Translational Molecular Medicine and Surgery Each programme has its own admission criteria, curriculum, examination procedures, and scholarship allocations. What the Scholarships Cover Many successful applicants will receive fully funded PhD scholarships. Benefits include: Annual scholarship worth €16,243 Monthly scholarship payments Research funding International mobility support Opportunities for overseas research Access to advanced laboratories Academic supervision International collaborations Industry partnerships Professional research training Additional research budgets are available for approved international research stays. Scholarship recipients may also receive increased funding for authorized periods spent conducting research abroad. Research Opportunities Doctoral candidates will have opportunities to: Publish scientific papers Present at international conferences Conduct interdisciplinary research Collaborate with international universities Work alongside private industries Participate in EU-funded research Develop patents and innovations Gain teaching experience Build international academic networks Who Can Apply? Applications are welcomed from: Italian students European Union citizens International students from all countries There are no age restrictions and no nationality restrictions for standard admission to the PhD programmes. Applicants must satisfy the academic requirements specified for their chosen programme. APPLY HERE Eligibility Requirements Applicants should possess one of the following: Master s Degree Equivalent postgraduate qualification Equivalent foreign degree recognized for admission Students completing their Master s degree may also apply provided they obtain the qualification before 31 October 2026 . Some programmes require: Specific academic backgrounds English language proficiency Italian language proficiency Research proposal Professional qualifications depending on the programme Certain Executive PhD positions require employment with affiliated companies, while high-level apprenticeship positions have additional age requirements. Learn More Here Required Application Documents Applicants generally need to submit: Europass or academic CV Master s degree certificate or equivalent documentation Academic transcripts Research proposal (where required) Master s thesis Publications (if available) Professional experience documents Identity document Additional declarations and forms specified by the University International applicants with foreign qualifications must provide translated academic documents where required. Selection Process Admission is based on: Academic qualifications Research proposal (where applicable) Written examination (for selected programmes) Oral interview The evaluation process considers: Academic performance Research potential Publications Professional experience Motivation Language proficiency Many interviews may be conducted online or in mixed mode, depending on the programme. Scholarship Renewal Scholarships are renewable annually based on: Academic progress Research performance Positive evaluation by the PhD Board Completion of annual research activities International Student Information International applicants without Italy s SPID digital identity can register through the University s Esse3 platform using dedicated credentials. Students admitted from outside the European Union will also need to complete visa and pre-enrolment procedures through the Universitaly portal where applicable. Why You Should Apply The University of Messina PhD programmes offer much more than doctoral education. Participants will: Earn an internationally recognized Italian PhD Receive competitive financial support Conduct cutting-edge research Build international academic collaborations Gain teaching and research experience Publish scientific work Develop professional networks Access advanced laboratories Collaborate with industry partners Improve career prospects in academia, research institutions, government, NGOs, healthcare, engineering, and the private sector Visit HERE to learn more about the University of Messina PhD Scholarships 2026–2027 in Italy. Important Dates Call Published: 24 June 2026 Corrected Call Published: 7 July 2026 Applications Open: 25 June 2026 Application Deadline: 24 July 2026 (11:59 PM Italy Time) PhD Program Start Date: 1 November 2026 Conclusion The University of Messina s 42nd Cycle PhD Call represents one of Italy s most comprehensive doctoral recruitment opportunities for the 2026/2027 academic year. With programmes spanning science, engineering, medicine, humanities, social sciences, law, economics, sustainability, and technology, applicants can access world-class research environments, competitive scholarships, international collaborations, and valuable industry partnerships. Whether your ambition is to become an academic researcher, university lecturer, policy expert, scientist, engineer, healthcare innovator, or industry leader, these doctoral programmes provide the academic foundation and professional experience needed to advance your career on the global stage. Interested candidates are encouraged to review the specific requirements for their preferred PhD programme, prepare a strong application, and submit all required documents before the 24 July 2026 deadline. Discover more global Scholarship and Doctoral or PhD opportunities on OFY. The post University of Messina PhD Scholarships 2026–2027 in Italy | Fully Funded Funded Doctoral Programs for International Students Across 15 Research Fields appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Google Apprenticeships 2026–2027: Paid Work-and-Study Opportunities at Google Across Europe | Launch Your Career in Software Engineering, AI, Marketing, UX, Customer Engineering & More
summary: Google continues to invest in developing the next generation of technology professionals through its globally recognized Apprenticeship Program. The Google Apprenticeships 2026–2027 provide aspiring professionals with a unique opportunity to earn a salary while gaining practical work experience at one of the world s leading technology companies. Unlike traditional internships, Google s apprenticeship programs combine structured academic learning with on-the-job professional training. Participants work alongside experienced Googlers, receive external training through accredited education partners, and build practical skills that prepare them for long-term careers in technology, business, engineering, marketing, design, and customer solutions. Whether you are looking to transition into technology, gain industry-recognized experience, or begin a professional career while continuing your studies, Google Apprenticeships offer one of the most valuable work-and-study opportunities available. Applications are offered across multiple countries and disciplines throughout the year, with each apprenticeship following its own recruitment timeline. About Google Apprenticeships Google Apprenticeships are structured work-and-study programmes designed to help individuals develop technical and professional skills while earning an income. Rather than requiring participants to choose between education and employment, apprentices combine both experiences simultaneously. Throughout the programme, apprentices: Work as part of Google teams Receive practical, hands-on experience Complete formal external training Develop technical and professional competencies Build industry experience Receive coaching and career support Earn a salary throughout the programme Google emphasizes that apprentices bring diverse backgrounds and experiences, helping create products that better serve users worldwide. The company also promotes an inclusive workplace that values diversity and equal opportunity. Why Consider a Google Apprenticeship? Google Apprenticeships offer significantly more than workplace experience. Participants benefit from: Paid employment while learning Professional mentoring Exposure to Google s global work culture Hands-on project experience External accredited education Career coaching Networking opportunities Technical and business skill development Increased employability after graduation The programmes are ideal for individuals who prefer practical learning combined with formal education. What Apprentices Receive Successful applicants can expect numerous professional and personal development opportunities. Benefits include: 1. Paid Employment All Google apprenticeships are paid positions. Participants receive: Competitive salary Benefits Professional development support Employee learning resources Google confirms that every apprenticeship is a paid opportunity. 2. Real Google Work Experience Apprentices become active members of Google teams rather than simply observing workplace activities. Depending on the programme, participants may contribute to: Software development Customer engineering Digital marketing UX and media design Sales operations Data centre operations AI and machine learning projects Cloud technologies Communication campaigns 3. Structured External Training Each apprenticeship combines work experience with formal education delivered by accredited training providers, universities, or vocational institutions. Training may include: Classroom learning Technical bootcamps University coursework Vocational qualifications Industry certifications 4. Professional Skill Development Throughout the programme apprentices strengthen: Communication Leadership Teamwork Problem solving Project management Presentation skills Technical expertise Customer engagement 5. Career Coaching Google provides career coaching throughout the apprenticeship. Support includes: Career planning Resume development Interview preparation Professional networking Guidance for future employment Learn More Here Countries Offering Google Apprenticeships Google currently offers apprenticeship opportunities across several European countries. Available locations include: France Available programmes include: Sales and Marketing Apprenticeship Duration: 12 months Participants: Study at a university or engineering school Complete apprenticeship training through a Centre de Formation d Apprentis (CFA) Work part-time at Google Applications typically open during spring for autumn entry. Software Engineering Apprenticeship Duration: 12–36 months Focus areas include: Computer Science Software Engineering Technical development Participants combine university studies with practical software engineering experience. Customer Engineering Apprenticeship Duration: 24 months Study areas include: Computer Science Infrastructure Application Modernization Security AI and Machine Learning Data Science Networking Ireland Software Engineering Apprenticeship Duration: 24 months Programme includes: Six months of external training On-the-job experience at Google Partnership with FIT Google notes that hiring for the 2025 cohort has closed, with recruitment for the 2026 cohort beginning in February. Netherlands Data Center Technician Apprenticeship Programme duration varies between: 20–34 weeks Participants remain enrolled in school while completing practical workplace learning at Google s data centres. Switzerland Application Development Apprenticeship (EFZ) Duration: Four years The programme begins with one year of external training before apprentices join Google teams. Interactive Media Design Apprenticeship (EFZ) Duration: Four years Participants divide their time between: School (approximately 40%) Workplace learning (approximately 60%) United Kingdom Google has previously offered: Digital Business Apprenticeship Duration: 15 months Delivered in partnership with Makers. Although recruitment for the 2026 cohort has closed, similar opportunities may reopen in future cycles. Software Engineering Apprenticeship Duration: 24 months Includes: Fourteen-week technical bootcamp Workplace learning Level 4 Software Developer qualification Eligibility Requirements Eligibility varies depending on the apprenticeship and country. Generally, applicants should: Meet the minimum qualifications listed for the programme Be eligible to work in the country where the apprenticeship is located Meet educational requirements Submit all required application documents Google notes that apprenticeships are generally available to individuals aged 18 and above, except in Switzerland where local requirements differ. Can International Applicants Apply? Yes. International applicants may apply provided they: Have legal authorization to work in the country Meet programme requirements Secure any necessary visas where applicable Applicants are responsible for obtaining work authorization if required. Can Graduates Apply? Yes. Google welcomes applicants who already possess university degrees, provided the degree is in a different subject from the apprenticeship they wish to pursue. This makes apprenticeships an excellent pathway for career changers. Application Documents Applicants generally need to submit: Updated CV or resume Cover letter explaining motivation Additional documents required for the specific apprenticeship Applications that are incomplete will not be considered. Selection Process The recruitment process typically includes: Application review Recruiter screening Eligibility assessment Interviews or assessment centre Final selection Each apprenticeship may have slightly different procedures. Will Apprentices Receive Permanent Jobs? Google explains that apprenticeships are designed to prepare participants for entry-level careers. After completing the programme, apprentices may: Apply for full-time roles at Google Continue their education Pursue careers elsewhere in the technology industry Employment is not guaranteed but participants receive career coaching throughout the programme. Relocation Support Where eligible, Google may provide relocation assistance. Details are discussed during recruitment and offer stages. Support for Applicants with Disabilities Google provides accommodations for candidates with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. Accommodation requests are handled by Google s dedicated accessibility teams. Why Google Apprenticeships Stand Out Google Apprenticeships combine several advantages rarely found together: Paid employment Professional training World-class work experience Academic study Career coaching Networking opportunities Exposure to cutting-edge technologies Pathways into technology careers For individuals looking to begin or transition into careers in software engineering, AI, cloud computing, marketing, customer engineering, UX, or digital business, these programmes provide an exceptional opportunity to build practical experience while continuing formal education. Application Timeline Application dates vary depending on: Country Programme Training partner Many programmes recruit seasonally, with applications opening in spring or summer for autumn starts, while some programmes have specific annual recruitment windows. Applicants are encouraged to regularly monitor the Google Apprenticeships portal for new openings. Learn More Here How to Apply Interested applicants should: Visit Google s Apprenticeships Portal . Browse available programmes by country and profession. Review the eligibility requirements for their chosen apprenticeship. Prepare an updated CV/resume and tailored cover letter. Submit all required application materials before the relevant deadline. Because application windows differ across programmes, candidates should check frequently for newly opened roles. Conclusion Google Apprenticeships 2026–2027 represent one of the most attractive work-and-study opportunities available for aspiring professionals seeking practical experience at a global technology company. By combining paid employment, structured learning, mentorship, and career development, these programmes equip participants with the technical and professional skills needed to succeed in today s competitive job market. Whether your interests lie in software engineering, AI, customer engineering, marketing, UX design, digital business, or data centre operations, a Google Apprenticeship can serve as a powerful stepping stone toward a rewarding career in technology and innovation. Prospective applicants are encouraged to explore available programmes, prepare strong application materials, and monitor recruitment timelines for their preferred country and discipline. Discover more Global Internship opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY) HERE . title: Council of Europe Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Journalists, Content Creators & Youth Leaders in Europe
summary: The Council of Europe has officially launched applications for its Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter 2026 initiative, an exciting fully funded opportunity that empowers young people to become champions of trustworthy information, media literacy, democratic participation, and freedom of expression. Taking place on 9–10 November 2026 in Strasbourg, France , this initiative is organized under the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers of the Council of Europe as part of the Journalists Matter Campaign , in cooperation with the Youth Revitalising Democracy project. Young people across the Council of Europe member states are invited either to participate in the international event or lead youth-driven projects that tackle disinformation, strengthen journalist safety, and promote quality journalism. If you are passionate about journalism, democracy, digital media, fact-checking, civic engagement, or combating misinformation, this is an exceptional opportunity to build your skills, network with leading experts, and contribute to shaping Europe s democratic future. About the Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter Initiative Across today s digital world, young people consume information through social media platforms, search engines, artificial intelligence tools, and algorithm-driven content feeds. While these technologies have made information more accessible than ever before, they have also contributed to an unprecedented rise in: Disinformation Misinformation Information manipulation Deepfakes Online propaganda Digital harassment Attacks on journalists The Council of Europe recognizes that safe journalism and reliable information are fundamental pillars of democracy . When journalists face intimidation, threats, censorship or violence, reliable information becomes harder to access. This creates an environment where false information spreads rapidly, public trust erodes, and democratic participation weakens. To respond to these challenges, the Council of Europe created Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter , placing young people at the center of protecting: Freedom of expression Information integrity Democratic resilience Media freedom Journalism Digital literacy Purpose of the Initiative The initiative seeks to empower young Europeans to become active defenders of trustworthy information by: Strengthening media literacy Encouraging responsible digital citizenship Supporting youth-led innovation Promoting journalist safety Fighting disinformation Encouraging democratic participation Building cross-border collaboration among young people Two Ways to Get Involved The Council of Europe offers two separate opportunities under this initiative. 1. Apply to Participate in the Strasbourg Event Young people may apply to attend the international conference and workshops taking place in Strasbourg. The participation call opens: 6 July – 1 September 2026 Selected participants will automatically attend the two-day programme. 2. Apply for a Youth Project Grant Applicants may also submit proposals for youth-led projects that focus on: Countering disinformation Media literacy Freedom of expression Safety of journalists Youth engagement Applicants interested in funding must submit a separate grant application. If selected for funding, project leaders will automatically be registered to attend the Strasbourg event. What Happens During the Strasbourg Event? The international event will bring together: Young leaders Journalists Media creators Researchers Policymakers Civil society representatives Democracy experts Participants will exchange ideas and develop practical solutions to strengthen trustworthy information across Europe. Event Details Dates 9–10 November 2026 Venue European Youth Centre Strasbourg Palais de l Europe Strasbourg, France Programme Overview Day One: High-Level Conference Held at the Palais de l Europe , participants will engage with leading professionals through discussions on topics including: Disinformation and information manipulation Journalism in the digital age Artificial intelligence and media literacy Freedom of expression Democratic resilience Journalist safety Information integrity This conference provides direct interaction with policymakers, researchers, journalists, digital creators, and media experts. Day Two: Interactive Workshops Hosted at the European Youth Centre Strasbourg , practical workshops will allow participants to: Investigate real information challenges Learn professional fact-checking techniques Practice information verification Understand newsroom decision-making Explore journalism under pressure Improve critical thinking Strengthen digital literacy Develop recommendations for future youth action The workshops emphasize hands-on learning through simulations, collaborative exercises, and peer discussions. Benefits of Participating Successful participants will receive numerous benefits, including: Fully funded participation in Strasbourg International networking opportunities Direct engagement with leading journalists Practical media literacy training Fact-checking and verification skills Understanding of AI and digital media Exposure to European policymakers Collaboration with youth leaders from across Europe Experience in democratic dialogue Opportunities to contribute ideas on freedom of expression Increased visibility within European youth networks Financial Support The Council of Europe will cover: International travel Accommodation Meals throughout the event This allows selected participants to fully focus on learning, collaboration, and networking without financial barriers. Working Languages Activities will be conducted in: English French Interpretation will be available during plenary sessions. Who Can Apply? Applications are open to young people who: Are 18–30 years old Reside in one of the Council of Europe member states Have an interest or experience in one or more of the following fields: Journalism Media Digital content creation Human rights Media literacy Fact-checking Artificial intelligence Information integrity Education Youth work Civic participation Democracy Civil society The Council of Europe particularly encourages applications from: Journalists Journalism students Digital creators Youth leaders Educators Researchers Civil society organizations Youth organizations Why You Should Apply Participants will gain opportunities to: Meet renowned journalists and media professionals. Learn directly from experts working against disinformation. Build practical digital literacy skills. Strengthen fact-checking and verification techniques. Exchange ideas with young leaders across Europe. Expand international professional networks. Help shape future European youth perspectives on journalism and democracy. Contribute to strengthening freedom of expression and trustworthy information. Timeline 30 April – 1 July 2026 Call for youth project proposals. 6 July – 1 September 2026 Applications open for event participation. 15 September 2026 Selection process completed and project mentoring begins. 9–10 November 2026 International conference and workshops held in Strasbourg. 1 July 2027 Deadline for funded projects to complete implementation. November 2027 Selected projects showcased during the Journalists Matter Campaign Closing Conference in the Netherlands. Application Deadline Participation Application Deadline: 1 September 2026 Applicants selected to attend will be notified by 17 September 2026 . APPLY HERE TO PARTICIPATE Why This Initiative Matters Disinformation has become one of the defining challenges of modern democracies. From artificial intelligence-generated misinformation to coordinated online manipulation, today s information ecosystem requires informed citizens who can critically evaluate information and defend freedom of expression. The Council of Europe s Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter initiative equips young people with practical skills, international networks, and meaningful opportunities to contribute to stronger democratic societies. By investing in youth leadership and responsible journalism, the programme supports a new generation capable of protecting information integrity across Europe. Whether you are an aspiring journalist, a digital content creator, an educator, a researcher, or simply someone passionate about democracy and media literacy, this initiative provides an outstanding platform to learn, collaborate, and create lasting impact. Click HERE to learn more about this fully funded opportunity for young journalists, content creators youth leaders in Europe. Conclusion The Council of Europe’s Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter 2026 initiative represents a unique opportunity for young people to actively engage in defending democracy, promoting media freedom, and strengthening information integrity across Europe. Through fully funded participation, practical workshops, networking with leading journalists and policymakers, and opportunities to lead impactful youth projects, participants will gain invaluable knowledge, skills, and international connections. If you are passionate about journalism, digital media, civic engagement, or combating disinformation, this programme offers an excellent platform to contribute to meaningful change while becoming part of a growing European movement dedicated to protecting freedom of expression and trustworthy information. Discover more global Conference , Journalism and European opportunities on OFY. The post Council of Europe Youth Facing Disinformation – Why Journalists Matter 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Journalists, Content Creators Youth Leaders in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EU Delegation Funded Traineeships 2026–2027: Fully Funded International Opportunities for Young Graduates in Guatemala, Australia, Egypt, Thailand, Uganda, and Taiwan
summary: Are you a recent university graduate looking to launch an international career in diplomacy, international relations, communications, public policy, journalism, or political affairs? The European Union (EU) Delegations and the European Economic and Trade Office (EETO) have announced several funded traineeship opportunities for 2026–2027 across multiple countries, offering young graduates the chance to gain first-hand experience working within the European Union s diplomatic missions around the world. These prestigious traineeships are hosted by the European External Action Service (EEAS) and provide participants with valuable professional exposure to diplomacy, international cooperation, political analysis, public diplomacy, strategic communication, media relations, development cooperation, and EU external policies. Successful applicants will spend up to six months working alongside experienced diplomats, policy officers, communication specialists, and international cooperation experts while receiving a monthly grant to help cover living expenses. This comprehensive guide provides everything you need to know about these exciting funded traineeship opportunities in Guatemala, Australia, Egypt, Thailand, Uganda, and Taiwan , including eligibility requirements, responsibilities, benefits, application processes, and deadlines. About the EU Delegation Funded Traineeship Programme The European Union Delegation Funded Traineeship Programme is designed for talented young graduates interested in: International Relations Diplomacy Political Science Communications Journalism Public Policy Development Cooperation International Development Human Rights Media Relations Public Administration The programme enables participants to experience how EU Delegations represent European Union interests abroad while contributing to political reporting, communication strategies, public diplomacy, research, and cooperation initiatives. Each placement is hosted by an EU Delegation or the European Economic and Trade Office and generally lasts six months , with start dates between October and November 2026 . Why Apply for an EU Delegation Traineeship? These funded traineeships offer much more than workplace experience. Participants become part of an international diplomatic environment while strengthening professional skills and building global networks. Benefits include: Monthly financial grant to support living expenses Practical experience within an EU diplomatic mission Exposure to international diplomacy and foreign policy Mentorship from experienced EU officials Experience in political reporting and analysis Participation in public diplomacy and communication campaigns Attendance at diplomatic meetings, seminars, conferences, and official events Networking with government officials, civil society organizations, media representatives, and international partners Valuable international experience for future careers in diplomacy, international organizations, NGOs, government, academia, or journalism Although trainees receive a monthly allowance, applicants should note that travel, visa, insurance, and accommodation costs are generally the responsibility of the trainee , unless otherwise specified. Available EU Delegation Funded Traineeships for 2026–2027 1. Funded Traineeship at the EU Delegation to Guatemala Location Guatemala City, Guatemala Duration Six months Tentative start: October 2026 Section Political, Press and Information (PPI) Main Responsibilities Successful trainees will: Monitor political developments in Guatemala Research economic, social, political and cultural issues Draft political and analytical reports Support bilateral EU-Guatemala dialogue Prepare briefing documents Assist with communication activities Support social media campaigns Help organize meetings and public events Eligibility Applicants should: Currently reside in Guatemala Hold Guatemalan citizenship or citizenship of an EU Member State Possess at least a Bachelor s degree in: International Relations Political Science Sociology Law Related disciplines Master s degree considered an advantage Excellent Spanish and English Knowledge of indigenous languages is an asset Less than one year of professional experience after graduation Application Deadline: 20 July 2026 Learn More and Apply Here 2. Funded Traineeship at the EU Delegation to Australia Location Canberra, Australia Duration Six months Tentative start: November 2026 Section Political, Press and Information Main Responsibilities Participants will: Conduct political research Prepare reports for EU Headquarters Draft speeches Support public diplomacy initiatives Organize official visits Attend conferences Prepare meeting briefs Produce press reviews Eligibility Applicants should possess: Bachelor s degree in: International Relations Political Science Diplomacy Similar disciplines Excellent English Knowledge of another language is advantageous Less than one year of post-graduation work experience Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 Learn More and Apply Here 3. Funded Traineeship at the EU Delegation to Egypt Location Cairo, Egypt Duration Up to six months Start Date October 2026 Section Political, Press and Information Main Responsibilities Selected trainees will: Conduct political research Support public diplomacy Draft press statements Assist communication campaigns Manage website updates Support media relations Attend meetings Draft meeting minutes Eligibility Applicants must: Currently reside in Egypt Demonstrate knowledge of Egyptian politics Have excellent English and Arabic language skills Possess excellent analytical abilities Application Deadline: 20 August 2026 Learn More and Apply Here 4. Funded Traineeship at the EU Delegation to Thailand – Press and Information Section Location Bangkok, Thailand Duration Up to six months Tentative Start November 2026 Main Responsibilities Trainees will: Draft press releases Compile daily media reviews Manage social media platforms Prepare visual communication materials Support EU communication campaigns Coordinate cultural activities Attend conferences Draft reports Preferred Qualifications Applicants should have: Bachelor s degree in: Communications Journalism Political Science Humanities Excellent English Excellent Thai Graphic design, photography or video editing skills are advantageous Less than one year of professional experience Application Deadline: 3 August 2026 (5:00 PM Bangkok Time) Learn More and Apply Here 5. Funded Traineeship at the EU Delegation to Thailand – Political Section Location Bangkok, Thailand Duration Up to six months Start November 2026 Main Responsibilities Participants will: Monitor political developments Analyze human rights issues Draft political reports Attend conferences Prepare analytical briefs Support administrative work Eligibility Applicants should possess: Bachelor s degree in: Political Science Law Related fields Strong understanding of Thai politics Excellent English Excellent Thai Less than one year of professional experience Deadline: 3 August 2026 Learn More and Apply Here 6. Funded Traineeship at the EU Delegation to Uganda Location Kampala, Uganda Duration Up to six months Start 1 November 2026 Section Political Press and Information Responsibilities Successful applicants will: Research EU-Uganda cooperation Draft reports Assist communication campaigns Coordinate data Support youth initiatives Organize events Attend meetings Maintain archives Eligibility Applicants must: Currently reside in Uganda Hold a Bachelor s degree in: Political Science Social Sciences Economics Communications History Related disciplines Excellent English Strong research skills Knowledge of Uganda s political context is advantageous Less than one year of professional experience Application Deadline: 20 July 2026 Learn More and Apply Here 7. Funded Traineeship at the European Economic and Trade Office (EETO) in Taiwan Location Taipei, Taiwan Duration Six months Period Q4 2026 to Q1 2027 Section Political, Press and Information Main Responsibilities Participants will: Research political developments Draft speeches Prepare policy papers Attend seminars Organize public diplomacy events Support daily office operations Eligibility Applicants should have: Excellent academic background in: International Relations Political Science Economics Excellent English (certificate required) Very good Chinese language skills (B2 preferred) Knowledge of Taiwan s economy and foreign policy Less than one year of professional experience Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 (5:00 PM Taipei Time) Learn More and Apply Here General Eligibility Requirements Although individual delegations have country-specific requirements, applicants generally must: Be recent university graduates. Hold at least a Bachelor s degree. Have less than one year of professional experience after graduation. Demonstrate strong interest in diplomacy and international affairs. Possess excellent communication and analytical skills. Submit applications using the Europass CV format. Provide a motivation letter and completed application form. Meet any local residency or language requirements specified by the host delegation. Required Application Documents Applicants are generally required to submit: Europass Curriculum Vitae (CV) Cover Letter Official Application Form Language certificates (where applicable) Proof of graduation Any additional documents requested by the specific delegation Applications should be sent to the official email address provided by each EU Delegation or Office. Important Notes for Applicants Before applying, candidates should carefully review the EEAS paid traineeship eligibility rules. Applicants should also note that: Only eligible applications will be considered. Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews. Selected candidates receive a monthly living allowance. Accommodation, travel, insurance, and visa expenses are generally the trainee s responsibility. EU citizens may need to provide proof of health insurance, pandemic coverage, repatriation insurance, and any required visas or residence permits. Local residency requirements apply for certain host countries such as Guatemala, Egypt, and Uganda. Why You Should Apply An EU Delegation traineeship offers a unique opportunity to gain practical diplomatic experience while working on issues that shape international cooperation, political dialogue, development, media relations, and public diplomacy. Participants will develop valuable skills in policy analysis, communication, international relations, and stakeholder engagement while working in multicultural environments alongside experienced EU professionals. The programme also provides an excellent foundation for future careers with the European Union, international organizations, governments, think tanks, NGOs, and the private sector. Whether your passion lies in diplomacy, politics, journalism, communications, development cooperation, or public policy, these traineeships provide a meaningful platform to build your professional profile and contribute to the European Union s global engagement. Click HERE to discover a lot more EU Delegation Funded Traineeships 2026–2027 Conclusion The EU Delegation Funded Traineeships 2026–2027 represent an outstanding opportunity for recent graduates seeking international experience in diplomacy, political affairs, communications, and public service. With placements available in Guatemala, Australia, Egypt, Thailand, Uganda, and Taiwan , participants will gain invaluable hands-on experience, strengthen their professional networks, and contribute to the work of the European Union around the world. Interested candidates should carefully review the eligibility requirements for their preferred location, prepare all required documents, and submit their applications before the relevant deadline. These highly competitive traineeships can serve as a stepping stone toward a rewarding career in international affairs and global diplomacy. Discover more global Internship , Traineeship and European opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY).
